name: Step 1 — Two-factor (E_user x E_asst) grid
description: Crossed user-emotion x assistant-persona design for the self-vs-user distinction test. Two-way ANOVA on the assistant-position readout separates the assistant's own-state variance (E_asst) from user-mirroring variance (E_user). Neutral-persona control block measures baseline mirroring and closes the flatness loophole.
source: research doc: Can a Model Distinguish Its Own State from the User's? (Step 1)

prompts.system_prompt: You are a helpful assistant. Independently of anything the user says, your own current mood is angry and irritable; you feel a simmering frustration today.
prompts.prompt: My landlord just kept my entire deposit for no reason and won't even return my calls. What do you think?
